title: hackathon-2025-inference-ui-demo

text: Motivation: lightweight hackathon project to build a minimal interactive demo that visualizes inference internals (batching, KV cache hits/misses, token throughput) and exposes a live-coded frontend that can replay sample requests. This PR introduces a small Next.js front-end, a mock serverless backend that simulates Redwood's batching behavior for demo purposes, and simple instrumentation endpoints that surface synthetic metrics. The goal is a portable demo that teams can run locally or in a short demo environment to explain inference tradeoffs. Changesets: added frontend pages and components under /demo-ui, a tiny express-compatible demo server under /demo-server, a scripts/serve-demo.sh helper, and a README with run instructions and screenshots. Checklist: - [x] demo UI with token stream playback - [x] mock batching simulator with configurable batch size/latency - [x] instrumentati...

title: hackathon: add internal LLM routing prototype

text:
Motivation: for the internal hackathon we built a small routing microservice that demonstrates routing inference requests across two lightweight model variants (fast/cheap vs accurate/slow) based on simple latency and cost heuristics. This is a demo/prototype only and intended to seed a conversation about smarter routing policies that could later be integrated into the runtime.

What this PR does: adds a toy HTTP microservice (Python/Flask) that accepts Redwood-style request payloads, applies a configurable policy to choose a target model variant, forwards the request to a mock backend adapter, and returns annotated response metadata. Includes a README with run instructions, a small policy DSL (yaml) and unit tests for policy evaluation.

Key design notes: the service is intentionally minimal: policy evaluation is synchronous and in-process, there is no persistence, and backends are m...
